chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Crl. Misc: Application No.S-242 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A No.3630/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection (s) 3. For hearing of main case 11.05.2026 None present on behalf of the applicant. No intimation is received. Applicant Shama has contended in this application that her daughter Jameela d/o Anwar Hussain, as stated in Paragraph-2 of this application, has been abducted by four proposed abductors. However, a perusal of the material available on record indicates that there is nothing to show that the applicant ever approached the law enforcement authorities. Further, the applicant has invoked her constitutional jurisdiction throughout the pleadings without availing the remedy before the Constitutional Bench of the High Court. Finally, the record reflects that the applicant has approached this Court directly, without first moving the Sessions Court, the forum before which an application for recovery of an alleged abductee ordinarily lies, particularly where the abductee is allegedly in the custody of private persons and within the jurisdiction of the Sessions court where the abductees reside, therefore, this application is not maintainable for want of jurisdiction, as well as on account of the applicant’s failure to exhaust the statutory remedy or to approach the law enforcement agency competent under the law. Given the above, this Crl. Misc: application is dismissed in limine along with listed applications. JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
